errors: The screening interval used in the `Colonoscopy` expression is set to 1 month (`Quantity` of 1 month), which is clinically incorrect for colon cancer screening (the guideline interval is 10 years). This causes the logic to incorrectly flag patients who have not had a colonoscopy in the last month as needing screening, rather than those who have not had one in the last 10 years.

The HbA1c laboratory test threshold is set to 9 g% – this unit is incorrect (HbA1c is expressed as a percentage, e.g., 9 %) and the threshold of 9 % is not a standard diagnostic cut‑off (the typical diagnostic threshold is 6.5 %).

The ELM implementation does not incorporate the 20 pack‑year smoking history requirement of the USPSTF guideline. The “Has Smoking History” expression only checks for an active Condition coded as “Tobacco User,” which captures current smokers but not former smokers or the pack‑year threshold. This omission makes the recommendation clinically incomplete.

The age threshold of ≥30 years is not consistent with current HIV screening guidelines, which recommend screening for adults aged 18–65 (or all adults). The logic also limits screening to once per year for all patients, whereas high‑risk populations may require more frequent testing. The recommendation text is generic and does not specify the recommended frequency or target population.

The BMI threshold for defining obesity is set to 35 kg/m², whereas standard clinical guidelines (e.g., WHO, ADA) define obesity as BMI ≥ 30 kg/m². This makes the screening criteria too restrictive. Additionally, the “Has Recent Glucose Test” interval is 1 year, while most guidelines recommend a 3‑year interval for routine prediabetes screening in high‑risk adults.

The age range in the ELM logic is 30–65 years, whereas the guideline (USPSTF) specifies screening for women aged 21–65 years. This lower‑bound mismatch makes the recommendation clinically unreasonable.
